title: Set up Retro68 toolchain locally
description: Build autc04/Retro68 from the clone at ~/github/Retro68 so m68k-apple-macos-gcc, the assembler, Rez, and Elf2Mac are on PATH. Verify with a hello-world build. Porting this repo's THINK C projects to Retro68 is a separate follow-up issue.

title: Source Apple Universal Interfaces 3.x for MacTCP support
description: Retro68 ships with Multiversal Interfaces, which omit MacTCP, OpenTransport, Carbon, Navigation Services, etc. FujiTests/TCPTests.c uses MacTCP. Locate Apple Universal Interfaces 3.x (3.4 preferred), drop into ~/github/Retro68/InterfacesAndLibraries/, rebuild Retro68. Required before TCPTests can build under Retro68.

title: Port FujiSerialStub to Retro68 as proof-of-concept
description: FujiSerialStub.c is the smallest target (tiny assembly name-stub driver). Write a CMakeLists.txt for it under the Retro68 toolchain, resolve any THINK_C-specific includes/macros in FujiCommon/FujiInterfaces.h that the stub touches, produce a .bin/.dsk and confirm it loads. Establishes the porting pattern for the larger drivers. Done: rewrote inline THINK C asm as standalone GAS .s in retro68/FujiSerialStub/, used .rsrcheader section trick to place DRVR header before Retro68 entry trampoline, Rez wraps as DRVR(-15903, .FujiStub, sysHeap+locked+preload). Build verified structurally; runtime test deferred to #8.

title: Set up Basilisk II + Quadra ROM for larger-target testing
description: Basilisk II is needed for: (a) running THINK C / CodeWarrior IDE inside the emulator if we ever want to build the original projects, (b) MacTCP / TCPTests work, (c) System 7.5+ testing beyond Mini vMac Plus's reach. Source Basilisk II macOS build (emaculation.com / kanjitalk755 fork), source a Quadra 650/800 1MB ROM, source a System 7.5.5 or 7.6.1 disk image, configure prefs, smoke-test by booting and running HelloWorld.APPL.

title: Port FujiSerialAsync (.Fuji driver) to Retro68
description: After #3 establishes the pattern, port the preferred async driver. Larger surface area: real device control entry, async I/O, dependency on FujiCommon/FujiFloppyInit.c and LedIndicators.h (assembly). Goal: produce a loadable driver binary that the test app can install. Scope (2026-05-18): build-only; real-driver harness wiring deferred to #10. 2026-05-18: build green. retro68/FujiSerialAsync/ now produces FujiSerialAsync.rsrc.bin = DRVR(-15904, .Fuji, sysHeap+locked+preload), flags 0x4F00, 5 dispatch entries, _VInstall A-trap embedded. Driver-header dispatch + VBL/completion glue extracted to driver_header.s and vbl_asm.s. LedIndicators inline asm replaced with a C framebuffer-poke fallback (led_indicators.c). FujiInterfaces.h gained a no-op GCC arm for Declare_LoMem. Awaiting user smoke test in Mini vMac before marking done.

title: Port FujiTests app to Retro68
description: Port the standalone test app (FloppyTests.c, SerialTests.c, TCPTests.c). TCPTests depends on issue #2 (Universal Interfaces / MacTCP). Floppy and Serial tests can land first. Reprioritised 2026-05-20: bumped low→medium now that #5 has landed and #10 will be the immediate next step.

title: Port FujiDeskAcc (Desk Accessory) to Retro68
description: Final and trickiest target: Desk Accessory packaging is non-trivial. Retro68 has Samples/MPWTool and SystemExtension that demonstrate non-application targets; check if a DA template exists or needs to be assembled by hand. Depends on the driver ports landing first. Reprioritised 2026-05-20: bumped low→medium — it is the user-facing endpoint of the port, and the driver story is now far enough along that this is the path to a shippable build.

title: Runtime-test ported drivers in Mini vMac
description: Build a tiny test harness that loads each ported DRVR resource into the unit table and exercises Open/Prime/Control/Status/Close. Needed to confirm the Retro68 builds behave identically to the THINK C originals at runtime, not just byte-for-byte at build time. Depends on at least one driver port (#3) being complete. Done: retro68/FujiStubTest builds FujiStubTest.APPL which loads DRVR(-15903), patches drvrHndl to a fake destination, directly invokes all 5 dispatch slots. Verified 5/5 PASS in Mini vMac on 2026-05-12. Device-manager-level testing (PBControl/etc by refNum) deferred to after #5 lands a real .Fuji to install.

title: Wire up LaunchAPPL for hands-off Retro68 test runs
description: LaunchAPPL (shipped with Retro68 toolchain) can drive Mini vMac headlessly: launch a built .APPL inside the emulator, capture stdout, auto-quit. Requires a System disk image, an 'autoquit' disk image (from gryphel.com), and a ~/.LaunchAPPL.cfg pointing minivmac-path/minivmac-rom/system-image/autoquit-image. Once configured, iterating on FujiStubTest (and future test apps) becomes a single shell command instead of a manual mount-and-double-click cycle. Reprioritised 2026-05-20: bumped medium→low and paused. Config is in place, but LaunchAPPL copies the minivmac binary into a per-run temp dir which breaks the macOS code signature; the spawned emulator never draws a window. Not blocking any other work — pick back up when iteration friction becomes the bottleneck. Status moved back to open.

title: Extend FujiStubTest to install + drive real .Fuji via OpenDriver/PBControl
description: Follow-up to #5 build-only scope. Now that FujiSerialAsync.rsrc.bin is loading cleanly, extend retro68/FujiStubTest to: (1) install the DRVR into the unit table via AddDrvr or by hand-stitching DCE, (2) OpenDriver(".Fuji", &refNum) and verify noErr, (3) issue PBControl/PBStatus/PBRead/PBWrite by refNum to exercise the dispatch path through the Device Manager rather than direct invocation. Covers the device-manager-level testing called out as deferred in #8. Reprioritised 2026-05-20: bumped medium→high — this is the immediate next step and the runtime-validation closer for the #5 driver port.
